confidence_reason: Direct GitHub Security Advisory records from the pnpm project identify affected package-manager versions, vulnerable behaviors, and fixed ranges for lockfile integrity, registry auth, Git dependency, alias traversal, patch traversal, and environment expansion issues.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: Repository-controlled pnpm install inputs, lockfiles, .npmrc files, patch files, Git dependencies, and registry metadata processed by vulnerable pnpm versions
payload_behavior: lockfile integrity verification can fail open or be repaired by default in specific scenarios
repository or registry controlled metadata can influence filesystem links, patch writes, Git fetch arguments, or registry credential destinations

open_questions: The reviewed direct advisories do not confirm exploitation in the wild or victim counts.

Practical exposure depends on whether vulnerable pnpm versions processed untrusted repositories, lockfiles, .npmrc files, registry metadata, Git dependencies, or patch files in a credential-bearing environment.

defender_takeaways.detection: Inventory pnpm versions and repository-controlled install inputs in source repositories, CI jobs, and developer workstations.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Prioritize jobs that run pnpm install on pull requests, forks, third-party source drops, or projects with repository .npmrc and patch files.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Upgrade pnpm to 10.34.2 or later on 10.x and 11.5.3 or later on 11.x, then review registry-auth logs and patch/lockfile changes during the vulnerable window.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Keep package-manager auth scoped to explicit registries, require frozen lockfiles for CI, and run untrusted install flows on isolated runners without long-lived secrets.

containment_complete: Credential-bearing CI jobs no longer run vulnerable pnpm versions against untrusted repository input.
eradication_complete: Source, runner, and Corepack configuration evidence show pnpm 10.34.2 or 11.5.3 or later.
recovery_complete: Registry auth logs and CI logs have been reviewed for repository-selected registry requests, traversal patch application, Git dependency fetch anomalies, and lockfile integrity changes.
closure_complete: Every pnpm install path has a documented version, input-trust boundary, and secret exposure decision.
